manifest.name: Quick Reads Sync

name: Quick Reads Sync

releases.author: mattbirchler
releases.description:
This is a maintenance release focused on code quality. **There are no changes to how the plugin behaves** — your settings, synced notes, and highlights are all unaffected, and updating requires no action on your part.

**Under the hood:**
- Improved compatibility with Obsidian pop-out windows by using the window-scoped timer for the startup sync.
- Tightened internal type safety when loading saved settings and rendering note templates.

These changes resolve several code-quality warnings from Obsidian's plugin review, helping the plugin meet the marketplace's higher standards.

releases.author: mattbirchler
releases.description:
## What's changed

- **Duplicate highlights are never displayed twice in a note.** Syncing now skips any highlight whose exact text already appears in the note, and duplicates within a single sync batch are collapsed.
- **New command: "Remove duplicate highlights from synced notes"** — a one-time cleanup that removes repeated identical highlights from existing notes (keeps the first copy, never touches your own notes/prose).
- Sync tracking is saved after each article, so an interrupted sync can no longer re-append highlights.
- Fetch results are deduped by id to guard against pagination overlap.
